subject: Physics
title: Neutrino Oscillations at Super-K
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Neutrino Oscillations at Super-K.

hook.text: In 1998, Japan's Super-Kamiokande detector proved that ghostly neutrinos change identity as they travel—meaning they have mass.

buildup.text: Neutrinos barely interact with matter. Trillions pass through your body every second without touching a single atom.

discovery.text: Super-K found fewer muon neutrinos coming from below than above. They were transforming into other types mid-flight.

twist.text: The Standard Model assumed neutrinos had zero mass. Oscillation proved they must have tiny masses—the model was incomplete.

climax.text: Takaaki Kajita and Arthur McDonald shared the 2015 Nobel Prize for proving neutrinos oscillate.

punchline.text: Ghost particles that shapeshift. Neutrinos proved the Standard Model wrong.

quiz.question: What do neutrino oscillations prove?
options: That neutrinos have mass, contradicting the original Standard Model
That neutrinos travel faster than light
